exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.1.5 — experience, impact, support, and information

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports tinnitus experience, duration, concerns, noise and stress context, hearing association, sleep impact, management discussion, individual preferences, information, and questions.

items.rationale: The exact section supports interval tinnitus experience, duration, concerns, hearing and noise context, sleep impact, preferences and discussion without asserting a cause. The support section allows recording a jointly documented management discussion, follow-up, and questions without creating advice.

items.final_wording: Supports documentation of quality-of-life, sleep, concentration, mental-health, behavioural, and psychological impact.

source.title: Tinnitus: assessment and management — Recommendations
source.population: Adults, children, and young people with tinnitus.
source.exclusions: Exact for tinnitus assessment, impact, referral, investigation, and support; not directly validated for UAE pathways.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.3.1–1.3.10 — functional, sleep, and psychological impact

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documentation of quality-of-life, sleep, concentration, mental-health, behavioural, and psychological impact.

items.rationale: The exact impact section supports documenting quality-of-life, sleep, concentration, mental-health and school or work impact plus clinician-performed assessment.

items.final_wording: Supports audiological assessment, selected tympanometry, neurological or otological context, and differentiated imaging review for pulsatile, unilateral, asymmetric, or otherwise concerning tinnitus.

added_items.rationale: The exact investigations section supports reviewing existing audiology, tympanometry, imaging or specialist results without ordering tests.
